name: Muse Spark 1.1
tagline: Meta Superintelligence Labs' agentic upgrade to Muse Spark — 1M-token context, multi-agent orchestration, and Meta's first paid API, released July 2026.
seoTitle: Muse Spark 1.1 — Meta's Agentic Reasoning Model
metaDescription: Muse Spark 1.1 is Meta Superintelligence Labs' upgrade to Muse Spark: a 1M-token multimodal reasoning model for agentic tasks, priced at $1.25/$4.25 per 1M tokens.
overview: Muse Spark 1.1 is Meta Superintelligence Labs' upgrade to the original Muse Spark, released on July 9, 2026. Meta positions it as a multimodal reasoning model built for agentic work — long-horizon coding, computer use, tool orchestration, and multimodal understanding — with major gains over the April 2026 Muse Spark on all four axes.

The model ships with a 1 million-token context window, adds multi-agent orchestration and computer use, and demonstrates zero-shot generalization to new native tools, MCP servers, and custom skills. It runs in Thinking mode inside the Meta AI app and on meta.ai for consumers, and opens the Meta Model API in public preview for developers — the first time Meta has charged for access to its own models.

API pricing is $1.25 per million input tokens and $4.25 per million output tokens, placing Muse Spark 1.1 in the same tier as Claude Haiku 4.5 and GPT-5.6 Luna. Meta's launch table shows Muse Spark 1.1 leading peers on tool-use and tool-augmented reasoning while trailing Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.6 on coding and multimodal benchmarks.
strengths: Leads Meta's published launch table on tool use (MCP Atlas 88.1), professional tool use (JobBench 54.7), and tool-augmented reasoning (Humanity's Last Exam with tools 62.1)
1M-token context window with active context management — remembers earlier actions, retrieves earlier work, and compacts to preserve critical steps
Multi-agent orchestration for long-horizon agentic tasks
Zero-shot generalization to new native tools, MCP servers, and custom skills
Priced at $1.25/$4.25 per million tokens — roughly a quarter of comparable Anthropic and OpenAI rates at similar tiers
useCases: Reach for it when you need a long-context agent that can drive tools, browsers, and computers across multi-step workflows.
Reach for it for large code migrations and bug fixes across big codebases where the 1M-token window and context compaction matter.
Reach for it when you want a lower-cost frontier-tier agentic model on the Meta Model API to prototype workflows against Claude Haiku 4.5 or GPT-5.6 Luna.

comparisonTable.caption: Muse Spark 1.1 vs peers, as published by Meta at launch (Muse Spark 1.1's leading rows highlighted; rivals shown in their strongest modes).

pricing.note: Meta Model API public preview pricing for US developers; the model is also available free in Thinking mode inside the Meta AI app and on meta.ai.

faq.a: Meta Superintelligence Labs released Muse Spark 1.1 on July 9, 2026. It is available in Thinking mode inside the Meta AI app and on meta.ai, and through the Meta Model API in public preview for US developers.

faq.q: How much does Muse Spark 1.1 cost?
faq.a: On the Meta Model API, Muse Spark 1.1 is priced at $1.25 per million input tokens and $4.25 per million output tokens — Meta's first paid model API. It is also available at no cost in Thinking mode via the Meta AI app and meta.ai.

faq.q: What is the context window of Muse Spark 1.1?
faq.a: Muse Spark 1.1 has a 1 million-token context window with active context management — the model remembers earlier actions, retrieves information from earlier work, and compacts context to preserve the critical steps needed later in a session.

faq.q: What is Muse Spark 1.1 best at?
faq.a: In Meta's launch comparison, Muse Spark 1.1 leads Claude Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.1 Pro on tool use (MCP Atlas 88.1), professional tool use (JobBench 54.7), and tool-augmented reasoning (Humanity's Last Exam with tools 62.1). It trails on coding (SWE-Bench Pro) and multimodal reasoning (BabyVision).
No. Like the original Muse Spark released in April 2026, Muse Spark 1.1 is proprietary and closed-weight — a break from Meta's open Llama line.

versionHistory.note: Adds multi-agent orchestration, 1M-token context management, computer use; Meta Model API in public preview at $1.25/$4.25 per 1M tokens.
First model from Meta Superintelligence Labs — small, fast, natively multimodal reasoner that leads on health.
